lines: Five days. Ten envelopes. A story written for you alone.
Each morning and each evening, a sealed letter arrives.
Your choices shape every word of it.
Read when I send. Answer honestly. Do completely.
I am always watching. I already know which one you'll reach for.

prologue.label: Day One · Morning
prologue.intro: You wake up. The apartment is quiet. You go to the kitchen and find something waiting on the counter—flowers, fresh cut, in a glass vase you don't remember owning. Beside it, a cream-coloured envelope with your name written across it in familiar handwriting.

choices.title: The Flowers
choices.hint: You notice them first. You always do.

card.heading: Take them. Touch the petals.
card.body: You pick up the vase. The flowers are real—expensive ones, the kind that don't come from the grocery store. You touch one petal and it's cool, slightly damp. There's a note underneath the vase on the counter.
card.rule: Open the envelope now.

choices.title: The Envelope
choices.hint: You know what it means.

card.heading: Don't touch the flowers yet.
card.body: You reach past the vase for the envelope instead. Your hand is steady. Your pulse is not. The envelope is heavier than it looks. Inside, you already know what you'll find—a letter written before dawn, for you to read now, while the kitchen is still dark enough to feel private.
card.rule: Open the envelope.

morning.label: Day One · Morning
morning.intro: You find the envelope where you expected it, but beside it is something else: a small box, neatly wrapped, waiting as if it has every right to be there. The room is quiet enough that every detail feels charged. Why these things, and why now? You do not know yet. That uncertainty is part of the thrill.

choices.title: A Length of Black Silk Ribbon
choices.hint: Soft, simple, and impossible to forget once it is there.

card.heading: Tie it around your wrist and keep it there.
card.body:
Black silk ribbon, cool and smooth between your fingers.

Tie it around your wrist before you leave the room. Wear it through the whole day. No one else needs to know why it is there.

Every time you notice it, let it pull your thoughts back to this morning and to the fact that someone chose this for you on purpose.
card.rule: Keep it on until tonight.

choices.title: A Delicate Chain Collar
choices.hint: It reads like jewelry to everyone else.

card.heading: Wear it where only you understand what it means.
card.body:
A fine chain with a small crimson stone. Elegant enough to pass as nothing more than jewelry, personal enough to feel different the moment it touches your skin.

Wear it through the day and let it become one of those details you keep reaching for without meaning to.
card.rule: Touch it when you want to remember why the day feels different.

choices.title: An Ankle Bracelet with a Little M
choices.hint: Small enough to hide. Personal enough to feel all day.

card.heading: Fasten it and let it become your private secret.
card.body:
At the bottom of the box is a delicate ankle bracelet with a tiny letter hanging from the clasp. It catches light only when it wants to.

Fasten it slowly. Feel the cool metal settle into place. Then go about your day as if nothing has changed, even though something clearly has.
card.rule: Leave it on until the evening envelope arrives.

evening.label: Day One · Evening
evening.intro: By evening, a new package has arrived. It is waiting for you as if it has been part of the plan all along. Whatever is inside is not asking anything difficult tonight. It is only asking you to choose a place, open the letter, and let the evening turn gently back toward you.

choices.title: The Bedroom
choices.hint: Quiet, soft light, and no reason to rush.

card.heading: Take the letter to the bedroom and settle in.
card.body:
Go to the bedroom, turn the lights low, and get comfortable. Open the letter there.

Read it slowly. Let yourself enjoy being thought of this carefully. Stay as long as you want. The point tonight is not pressure. The point is pleasure, privacy, and rest.
card.rule: Read. Breathe. Let the night be about you.

choices.title: The Couch
choices.hint: A softer kind of anticipation, built into an ordinary room.

card.heading: Take the letter to the couch and relax into it.
card.body:
Curl into the couch with the package beside you. Open the letter there, somewhere familiar enough to let your shoulders drop.

Read without hurrying. Let the day loosen around the edges. Enjoy the fact that the room is ordinary and the moment is not.
card.rule: Stay there until you feel yourself relax.

choices.title: Outside
choices.hint: Porch, patio, balcony, or anywhere the air changes the mood.

card.heading: Take the letter outside and read it in the evening air.
card.body:
Step outside with the package in your hands. Stand still for a moment before you open it.

Read the letter where the air is cooler and the world feels slightly farther away. Let yourself enjoy the quiet of being there with no one asking anything of you except to be present.
card.rule: Take your time. The moment does not need to be rushed.

envelopes.label: Day One · Morning
envelopes.intro: You find the envelope where you expected it, but beside it is something else: a small box, neatly wrapped, waiting as if it has every right to be there. The room is quiet enough that every detail feels charged. Why these things, and why now? You do not know yet. That uncertainty is part of the thrill.

choices.hint: Soft, simple, and impossible to forget once it is there.

card.body:

card.rule: Stay there until you feel yourself relax.

choices.title: Outside
choices.hint: Porch, patio, balcony, or anywhere the air changes the mood.

card.heading: Take the letter outside and read it in the evening air.
card.body:
Step outside with the package in your hands. Stand still for a moment before you open it.

Read the letter where the air is cooler and the world feels slightly farther away. Let yourself enjoy the quiet of being there with no one asking anything of you except to be present.
card.rule: Take your time. The moment does not need to be rushed.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 1 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Two · Morning
prologue.intro: At some point in the day, you realize something has been left for you somewhere you were not expecting to find it. A surprise. When you finally see them, the flowers look too carefully chosen to be random and too beautiful to mean nothing. For a moment you simply stand there, wondering what they are asking of you before the envelope says a word.

choices.title: Keep Them Visible
choices.hint: Let the reminder stay where the light can find it.

card.heading: Set them somewhere you will see them again and again.
card.body:
Put the flowers where the room keeps offering them back to you. Let them interrupt you gently all day.

Every glimpse should feel like a quiet nudge: this is still happening, and it was chosen for you with care.
card.rule: Leave them in the open until evening.

choices.title: Hide Them
choices.hint: Keep the surprise private and let it sharpen in secret.

card.heading: Put them somewhere only you will know to look.
card.body:
Move the flowers somewhere private and close the door behind them. Let the secret of them become part of the pleasure.

All day, you will know they are there. No one else will. That alone changes the atmosphere.
card.rule: Carry the secret with you until tonight.

morning.label: Day Two · Morning
morning.intro: Because you left the flowers where you could keep seeing them, the next envelope feels almost ceremonial. It asks you not to hurry the day, but to let it gather weight around you.

choices.title: Slowly - You Take Your Time
choices.hint: Let the anticipation build on purpose.

card.heading: Mark the day in three quiet pauses.
card.body:
Morning, afternoon, and just before evening, stop for one full minute. Touch the ribbon, the chain, or the bracelet you chose yesterday.

During each pause, notice what the day is doing to you. Let the anticipation lengthen instead of reaching past it.
card.rule: Keep one word for each pause. You will want them later.

choices.title: Directly - You Know What Works
choices.hint: No performance. Just clarity.

card.heading: Choose one detail and let it stay with you.
card.body:
Do not overcomplicate today. Pick one detail from the flowers, the room, or the note they came with and return to it whenever your thoughts drift.

Sometimes a day becomes charged because of one exact thing, not ten vague ones.
card.rule: By evening, know exactly which detail stayed with you longest.

choices.title: The Way I Would - Teasing
choices.hint: Stay just on the edge of knowing.

card.heading: Keep almost opening the next thought and then waiting.
card.body:
Each time you feel tempted to rush ahead and imagine the whole evening, stop short on purpose.

Let the anticipation stay unfinished. Let it hover. The not-quite is what makes the later moment land harder.
card.rule: Make yourself wait for the next envelope properly.

evening.label: Day Two · Evening
evening.intro: The evening envelope is heavier than the others. Inside is an address, a time, and just enough detail to make your pulse shift. La Paloma. Tomorrow. Just the two of us, and whatever the day becomes once you step into it.

choices.title: You Read It Twice
choices.hint: Part disbelief. Part anticipation.

card.heading: Read it again before you decide what you feel.
card.body:
You read the details once, then again, slower the second time. The place. The hour. The fact that this is real now.

It is not fear exactly. It is the thrill of realizing the story has moved another step closer to your actual life.
card.rule: Sleep on it. Tomorrow starts the moment you wake up.

choices.title: You Know What This Means
choices.hint: You smile before you mean to.

card.heading: Fold the note carefully and keep it with you.
card.body:
You do not need to read it twice to understand the shape of it. La Paloma. A real time. A real plan.

The smile comes first, then the nerves, then the satisfaction of knowing you are already in deeper than you were yesterday.
card.rule: Keep the note somewhere close until morning.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Morning
envelopes.intro: At some point in the day, you realize something has been left for you somewhere you were not expecting to find it. A surprise. When you finally see them, the flowers look too carefully chosen to be random and too beautiful to mean nothing. For a moment you simply stand there, wondering what they are asking of you before the envelope says a word.

choices.hint: Let the reminder stay where the light can find it.

card.body:

card.rule: Leave them in the open until evening.

choices.hint: Keep the surprise private and let it sharpen in secret.

card.body:

card.rule: Keep one word for each pause. You will want them later.

choices.hint: No performance. Just clarity.

card.heading: Choose one detail and let it stay with you.
card.body:

Sometimes a day becomes charged because of one exact thing, not ten vague ones.
card.rule: By evening, know exactly which detail stayed with you longest.

choices.title: The Way I Would - Teasing
choices.hint: Stay just on the edge of knowing.

card.heading: Keep almost opening the next thought and then waiting.
card.body:
Each time you feel tempted to rush ahead and imagine the whole evening, stop short on purpose.

Let the anticipation stay unfinished. Let it hover. The not-quite is what makes the later moment land harder.
card.rule: Make yourself wait for the next envelope properly.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Morning
envelopes.intro: Because you hid the flowers away, the next envelope arrives with a different kind of energy. It feels more private, more mischievous, as though the day now belongs to a secret only you are carrying.

choices.title: Slowly - Keep the Secret Warm
choices.hint: Let it follow you, quietly, everywhere.

card.heading: Return to the secret three times today.
card.body:
Three times today, pause long enough to remember exactly where you hid the flowers and how they looked in that room.

Do not go check on them each time. Let memory do the work. The distance sharpens it.
card.rule: By tonight, the hidden room should feel charged in your mind.

choices.title: Directly - Keep One Thought for Yourself
choices.hint: Choose a single thought and refuse to explain it.

card.heading: Carry one private thought through the whole day.
card.body:
Choose one thought that belongs only to you and the hidden flowers. Do not write it down. Do not say it aloud.

Let the pleasure of withholding it become part of the game.
card.rule: Keep that thought untouched until evening.

choices.title: The Way I Would - A Little Cruel, A Little Fun
choices.hint: Keep yourself guessing in the best way.

card.heading: Let the secrecy tilt the whole day.
card.body:
Every now and then, ask yourself whether hiding them made the day calmer or more electric. Do not answer too quickly.

The uncertainty is the point. You are not meant to settle into this one.
card.rule: Let the evening catch you slightly off balance.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Evening
envelopes.intro: The evening envelope is heavier than the others. Inside is an address, a time, and just enough detail to make your pulse shift. La Paloma. Tomorrow. Just the two of us, and whatever the day becomes once you step into it.

choices.title: You Read It Twice
choices.hint: Part disbelief. Part anticipation.

card.heading: Read it again before you decide what you feel.
card.body:
You read the details once, then again, slower the second time. The place. The hour. The fact that this is real now.

It is not fear exactly. It is the thrill of realizing the story has moved another step closer to your actual life.
card.rule: Sleep on it. Tomorrow starts the moment you wake up.

choices.title: You Know What This Means
choices.hint: You smile before you mean to.

card.heading: Fold the note carefully and keep it with you.
card.body:
You do not need to read it twice to understand the shape of it. La Paloma. A real time. A real plan.

The smile comes first, then the nerves, then the satisfaction of knowing you are already in deeper than you were yesterday.
card.rule: Keep the note somewhere close until morning.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Evening
envelopes.intro: This envelope feels different. No address at first. Just a small card, a folded note, and the sense that the night is smiling to itself. The instructions are playful, slightly crooked, and impossible to read without grinning.

choices.title: Follow the Note Exactly
choices.hint: You like the feeling of a plan closing around you.

card.heading: Keep the note. Do not smooth out the edges.
card.body:
The note gives you only fragments: a time tomorrow, a room name you recognize later, and one line that feels almost like a dare.

It is not dark so much as delightfully unfair. It leaves you curious on purpose and does not apologize for that.
card.rule: Do not look for more information tonight.

choices.title: Read It and Laugh Quietly
choices.hint: You can tell this one was written with a crooked smile.

card.heading: Let the mischief of it land.
card.body:
The message gives you less certainty and more mood. It tells you just enough to rearrange your evening and leave the rest humming at the edge of your attention.

Whatever tomorrow becomes, this version of the envelope wants you walking toward it slightly undone and enjoying that fact.
card.rule: Go to bed with one question still unanswered.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 2 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Three · Morning
prologue.intro: The next envelope does not begin with an instruction. It begins with a question, and the question lingers in the room after you read it. Before the day asks anything more of you, it wants the truth in your own words: how are you feeling now that this has become real enough to press against the shape of your actual life? Not the polished answer. Not the clever one. The true one.

choices.title: Tease Me
choices.hint: Keep the spark bright and playful.

card.heading: Tell me how the anticipation feels on you today.
card.body:
Write it plainly. Not for effect. Not to sound braver than you feel. Write the version that is true this morning: the nerves, the anticipation, the curiosity, the way the last two days keep catching you off guard when you should be thinking about something else.

The point is not confession for its own sake. The point is that the day is going to answer the feeling you name.
card.rule: Be honest enough that the next envelope can use it.

inputs.label: How do you feel right now
inputs.placeholder: A few honest words about the feeling you are carrying

choices.title: Make Me Wait
choices.hint: Stretch the feeling instead of resolving it.

card.heading: Name the part of waiting that has been hardest to ignore.
card.body:
Maybe it is curiosity. Maybe it is the fact that ordinary rooms have stopped feeling ordinary. Maybe it is the strange pressure of wanting the next envelope before the current one is even finished with you.

Whatever it is, say it clearly enough that the day can sharpen around it.
card.rule: Leave one sentence that the day can echo back to you later.

inputs.label: What has been hardest to ignore
inputs.placeholder: Write one sentence you would not want to lose

choices.title: Surprise Me
choices.hint: Hand the day a little uncertainty and see what it does with it.

card.heading: Tell me what you want more of tonight.
card.body:
Do not explain too much. Give the truth in a line or two and let the day build around it.

You are not being asked to control everything. You are being asked to participate honestly enough that the answer can come back feeling chosen, not generic.
card.rule: Answer in your own words. Let them matter.

inputs.label: What do you want more of tonight
inputs.placeholder: A line or two is enough

morning.label: Day Three · Morning
morning.intro: Because you answered honestly, the next envelope sounds closer now, more certain, as if it has stepped into the room with you. At nine o'clock tonight, you set your phone on a stand and call me on FaceTime. The camera stays on your face. I do not want performance. I want the truth of the moment as it catches up with you. The only choice left is how you want that tension guided once the call begins.

choices.title: Slowly - Let It Build
choices.hint: A quieter start, so the tension has somewhere to gather.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. Do not hurry the first minute.
card.body:
When the call begins, let the first moments stay quiet. Let the tension have somewhere to gather before either of you tries to force it into shape.

I want the change in your breathing. I want the moment your expression gives you away. Slowness is not restraint tonight. It is its own kind of intensity.
card.rule: Stay with the pace long enough for it to change the room.

choices.title: Follow My Pace
choices.hint: Let the rhythm of the call come from outside yourself for a while.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. Listen closely.
card.body:
You call, you listen, and you let the rhythm of the evening come from outside yourself for a while. The point is not to guess where it is going. The point is to follow closely enough that the guidance itself becomes the atmosphere.

That is part of what makes this path feel different: not uncertainty exactly, but the pleasure of not having to carry the whole shape alone.
card.rule: Do not rush ahead of the moment. Follow it.

choices.title: Keep Me Guessing
choices.hint: Leave room for the call to shift without warning.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. Let the rhythm change when it wants to.
card.body:
This path leaves room for shifts. The call can turn softer or sharper, slower or more playful, without warning. That uncertainty is part of what makes it electric.

You do not need to know the whole shape in advance. You only need to stay in it honestly enough for the changes to land.
card.rule: Let the unpredictability stay part of the pleasure.

evening.label: Day Three · Evening
evening.intro: The call is over, but the room does not feel empty yet. The evening envelope feels more personal than the earlier ones because it carries the shape of your own words back to you. What you wrote this morning is still somewhere under your skin, and now the night asks what you want to do with that feeling while it is still warm.

choices.title: Stay on the Call - Just Breathe
choices.hint: Do not rush out of the quiet immediately after.

card.heading: Stay for a minute or two longer. Let the silence do some of the talking.
card.body:
Do not end the call the second the important part is over. Stay there. Breathe. Let the quiet after the intensity have its own weight.

Sometimes the most revealing part of the night is the moment right after it changes you, when neither of you is trying to perform and the truth is simply there between you.
card.rule: Carry that version of yourself into tomorrow.

choices.title: The Mirror - After
choices.hint: Let your own reflection be part of the evening.

card.heading: Stand in front of the mirror and hold your own gaze.
card.body:
Lights low, one lamp if you want it. Stand in front of the mirror and look at yourself without hurrying away from the moment.

Touch the jewelry, ribbon, or bracelet you chose on Day One and remember how the whole week has been building from there. The point is not vanity. The point is recognition: this version of you did not arrive by accident.
card.rule: Tell yourself the truth once before you go to bed.

card.heading: Stand in front of the mirror and hold your own gaze.
card.body:
Lights low, one lamp if you want it. Stand in front of the mirror and look at yourself without hurrying away from the moment.

Touch the jewelry, ribbon, or bracelet you chose on Day One and remember how the whole week has been building from there. The point is not vanity. The point is recognition: this version of you did not arrive by accident.
card.rule: Tell yourself the truth once before you go to bed.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 3 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Four · Morning
prologue.intro: There is almost no writing in the morning envelope today. Just a time, a place, and the quiet confidence of a plan that expects you to follow it. Tonight is going to matter. The question is how you want to arrive at it.

choices.title: Arrive Early
choices.hint: Give yourself a few extra minutes to feel it coming.

card.heading: Get there before the moment officially begins.
card.body: Arrive a little early and let yourself settle. Use the extra time to notice the room, the light, the air, and what your body does when there is finally nothing left to anticipate except the actual meeting.
card.rule: Do not fill the silence. Let it work on you.

choices.title: Take the Long Way In
choices.hint: Let the anticipation build on the walk there.

card.heading: Do not hurry the approach.
card.body:
Whether it is the walk from the car, the hallway, or the path to the door, take the long way in if you can.

The point is to arrive already aware of yourself, already slightly changed by the fact that you are about to be met there.
card.rule: Let the approach be part of the scene.

choices.title: Text Me When You Arrive
choices.hint: One simple signal before anything else begins.

card.heading: Send a single message and wait.
card.body:
When you arrive, send one short message and do nothing else for a minute. No extra words. No nervous explanation.

Let that small pause become its own form of anticipation.
card.rule: Hold still long enough to feel the pause land.

evening.label: Day Four · Evening
evening.intro: Tonight is about touch and timing. No long speech. No elaborate setup. Just the intimacy of choosing how long to stay inside a kiss and what it leaves behind.

choices.title: Fifteen Minutes
choices.hint: Just long enough to feel it become impossible to forget.

card.heading: Fifteen minutes. Nothing else needs to interrupt it.
card.body:
The clock starts when the kiss begins. Fifteen minutes is short enough to feel almost unfair and long enough to change the whole evening.

By the end of it, both of you are more awake than you were at the start.
card.rule: Stop on time and let the wanting stay with you.

choices.title: Twenty-Five Minutes
choices.hint: Long enough to lose your sense of the room around you.

card.heading: Twenty-five minutes without rushing any part of it.
card.body:
This version takes its time. The kiss changes shape as it goes, starting soft, deepening, then slowing again just when it seems finished.

When it ends, the rest of the room feels quieter than before.
card.rule: Do not hurry the first minute or the last one.

choices.title: Forty Minutes
choices.hint: The kind of choice that turns the whole night luminous.

card.heading: Forty minutes of staying fully inside the moment.
card.body:
This is the choice for losing track a little. Forty minutes is long enough for the kiss to become its own atmosphere, something that folds the night around it.

Afterward, it will be difficult to think about anything else with the same clarity.
card.rule: Let the time disappear while you are in it.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 4 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

morning.label: Day Five · Morning
morning.intro: The morning envelope does not feel like local plans anymore. It opens onto a short trip: La Paloma, just you and me. No crowd, no audience, no performance. The pleasure of it begins before either of you arrives, in the fact that there is now a place attached to the story, a door that will close behind you, a stretch of day that belongs only to the two of you. From this point on, the week stops feeling abstract and starts feeling inevitable.

choices.title: The Drive
choices.hint: Let the anticipation build while the distance closes.

card.heading: Think about the drive before you think about the room.
card.body:
There is something about the drive to a place like La Paloma that changes the mood before anything even begins. The road becomes part of the threshold.

Let the anticipation start there, in motion, with the day steadily narrowing toward one place.
card.rule: Carry one thought from the drive into the evening.

inputs.label: What part of the trip feels most charged
inputs.placeholder: A detail you want the evening to remember

choices.title: The Arrival
choices.hint: The first moment of stepping into the place together.

card.heading: Imagine the arrival clearly.
card.body:
Think about the first minute at La Paloma: the way the place looks, the way the air shifts, the way a day becomes more intimate simply because it now has a location and a door that closes behind it.

The arrival matters. Sometimes it matters as much as anything after it.
card.rule: Keep one detail from the arrival in mind for later.

inputs.label: What do you notice first when you imagine arriving
inputs.placeholder: A detail you want held onto

choices.title: The Room
choices.hint: The private space where the day finally settles.

card.heading: Let the room become part of the tension.
card.body:
Think about the room itself: the quiet, the lighting, the fact that once you are there the outside world stops mattering for a while.

La Paloma is not just a destination. It is the container for the ending.
card.rule: Decide what feeling you want the room to hold first.

inputs.label: What should the room feel like first
inputs.placeholder: A line or two about the feeling you want to enter

evening.label: Day Five · Evening
evening.intro: At La Paloma, the last decision arrives with a different kind of weight. Up to this point the story has led you, surprised you, and watched you answer. Tonight it stops and asks one final question: does he choose the shape of the night, or does she? Either answer changes what comes next, but neither is casual. The room is already waiting. The question is whose hand is on the design of it.
evening.choicesIntro: Pick the path honestly. The next envelope will treat the answer as real.

choices.title: He Chooses
choices.hint: He sets the tone, the pacing, and the shape of the night.

card.heading: Let him author the details.
card.body:
This choice hands over the architecture of the evening. The room, the pacing, the palette, the first move, the final quiet afterward. It says: I want to be met by a plan that is already sure of itself.

Your role is not to overthink it. Your role is to arrive ready and let the night close around you in the form he chooses, without sanding down the edges before they can land.
card.rule: Leave room for surprise. The point is trust in the shape of it.

choices.title: She Chooses
choices.hint: She names the details and lets the night be built around them.

card.heading: Answer clearly enough for the evening to fit.
card.body:
If the night is going to reflect you, this is where you stop being vague. Choose the palette, the mood, the details that matter, the room-feeling you want to walk into, and the kind of ending that would make the whole trip feel chosen rather than generic.

The goal is not to be dramatic. The goal is to be accurate enough that the answer can become atmosphere.
card.rule: Choose carefully. The next envelope will take you seriously.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Morning
envelopes.intro: He chose. The day at La Paloma feels different now because there is nothing left for you to design, only to answer.

choices.title: I Follow The Plan
choices.hint: No edits. No softening. Just the shape that was chosen.

card.heading: Prepare simply and with intention.
card.body:
Do only what the day actually needs. Leave room for the evening to arrive as itself.

The point is not over-preparation. The point is readiness.
card.rule: By evening, arrive without trying to control what comes next.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Evening
envelopes.intro: The final envelope does not ask what you want. It tells you where to be, how to arrive, and what kind of evening this will become.
envelopes.choicesIntro: There is only one path here. The choice was yesterday.

choices.title: Follow Exactly
choices.hint: The authored ending. Deliberate, calm, and fully held.

card.heading: Let the plan close around the evening.
card.body:
The details are not accidental. The light, the pacing, the music, the distance between one moment and the next - all of it was chosen with care.

What matters now is not performance but recognition: the fact that the two of you are no longer improvising.
card.rule: Keep the room as it is for a little while longer afterward.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Morning
envelopes.intro: She chose. This morning is where you answer clearly enough for the final evening to fit you without guesswork.

choices.title: I Choose The Shape Of Tonight
choices.hint: Private. Specific. Entirely yours.

card.heading: Refine the atmosphere. The evening will follow.
card.body:
Choose clearly: the room, the atmosphere, the pace, and the kind of quiet you want after everything has landed.

This is not about exaggeration. It is about precision.
card.rule: By evening, choose the right night, not the loudest one.

inputs.label: Place
Atmosphere
Color palette
Pace
Featured element
Aftercare
What should the ending feel like
inputs.placeholder: Describe the ending in a few words

envelopes.label: Day Six · Evening
envelopes.intro: Tonight reflects what she chose. The room no longer feels generic. The mood no longer feels accidental. It was built with intention and it shows.
envelopes.choicesIntro: Pick the tone that comes closest to what was chosen earlier.

choices.title: Soft and Ceremonial
choices.hint: Tender, deliberate, private.

card.heading: Let the room stay quiet long enough to matter.
card.body: Everything slows down enough to feel distinct. The details land softly but precisely, and that precision is what makes the evening intimate instead of vague.
card.rule: Stay in the room after. Let the mood finish settling.

choices.title: Playful and Teasing
choices.hint: A smile inside the tension.

card.heading: Let the night move in and out of seriousness on purpose.
card.body: The evening keeps some lightness in it without ever becoming careless. The playfulness makes the intimacy brighter rather than smaller.
card.rule: Leave one small detail untouched until morning.

choices.title: Intense and Exacting
choices.hint: Structured, focused, and beautifully deliberate.

card.heading: Let precision become part of the care.
card.body: Nothing feels random in this version of the evening. The pacing is attentive, the details are chosen, and the quiet afterward feels earned rather than accidental.
card.rule: Write down the one detail you want to remember longest.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 5 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.
